response.justification: The candidate brings a long, versatile engineering career with genuine high-throughput distributed systems exposure (millions of requests/sec at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic), current hands-on AI-agent infrastructure work including MCP tooling, and strong TypeScript/Node depth — all well aligned with the AI-native environment. However, the role's must-have of strong Python is absent from both the skills list and work history (the systems work was in DLang), and evidence of staff-level technical strategy — directing decisions at scale and cross-functional stakeholder leadership — is thin; most roles read as senior IC delivery. Worth a conversation to probe Python depth and strategic scope, but not a clear yes.
strengths: Current hands-on AI-agent infrastructure and MCP/agentic-workflow tooling experience
Real high-throughput, low-latency distributed systems work (millions of HTTP req/sec)
Strong software craftsmanship background: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, testing tooling
concerns: No Python listed anywhere — a must-have; performance work was in DLang, a niche language
Limited demonstrated technical-strategy or cross-org stakeholder leadership at staff scope
No explicit operational/on-call or incident-response experience described
